data.title: Creative Realities Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Creative Realities ( (CREX) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Creative Realities, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors that it will delay its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company says the delayed filing relates specifically to its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report), not to other periodic reports, which remain current.

Management attributes the delay mainly to heavy post-deal workload tied to several late-2025 transactions. These include the acquisition of the CDM Business, a preferred stock private placement, and a refinancing of its credit facilities, all of which have consumed substantial internal resources.

The integration of the CDM Business and the complexity of the financing moves have slowed the company’s ability to deliver information needed for the year-end audit. They have also delayed completion of disclosure controls and the evaluation of internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2025.

Creative Realities expects to file its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-day extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. For investors, this signals a short, defined delay rather than an open-ended timing issue, though the company cautions that timing remains subject to execution risks.

The company warns that 2025 results will show significant changes versus 2024 because they will include two months of CDM Business operations and the impact of the financing transactions. However, it says it cannot yet provide reasonable quantitative estimates of these changes, framing all timing and impact comments as forward-looking statements subject to uncertainty.

Creative Realities notes that all other required filings over the past 12 months are up to date, indicating ongoing baseline compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed on March 30, 2026, by Chief Financial Officer Tamra Koshewa, underscoring management’s formal commitment to completing the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) and addressing the remaining audit and control work.

The most recent analyst rating on (CREX) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Creative Realities stock, see the CREX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CREX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CREX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (declining revenue, negative margins, negative operating cash flow). Technicals provide support with price above key moving averages, but momentum is nearing stretched levels. The earnings call adds moderate optimism due to the transformative CDM acquisition and synergy targets, while valuation remains pressured due to losses (negative P/E) and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on CREX stock, click here.

More about Creative Realities

Average Trading Volume: 72,878

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: FDCTech Delays 10-K Filing After Major Acquisition
data.description:
FDCTech ( (FDCT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FDCTech, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The company, which trades in financial technology and brokerage-related activities, is pushing back its annual filing while it completes work related to a major acquisition.

The main reason for the delay is the October 29, 2025 acquisition of 100% of Alchemy International Limited, a Seychelles-based FX/CFD broker-dealer licensed by the local Financial Services Authority. FDCTech is performing a U.S. GAAP audit of Alchemy International Limited for the 2024 fiscal year, reviewing its 2025 interim results, and consolidating those figures into FDCTech’s own financial statements.

Management says this consolidation work could not be finished by the original deadline without unreasonable effort or expense. As a result, the company is relying on the standard SEC extension that allows additional time to finalize and file its Form 10-K (Yearly Report), indicating that it expects to complete the filing within 15 calendar days after the prescribed due date.

FDCTech also signaled that investors should expect a significant change in its financial profile once the acquisition is fully reflected. The company states that the Alchemy International Limited deal will materially increase net assets by over $17 million, generate more than $13 million in revenues, and add over $6 million in net income for the period from the acquisition date through December 31, 2025.

These figures are effectively preliminary, forward-looking indications based on management’s current review and consolidation work. Actual reported results in the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) could differ once audits are completed and all accounting entries are finalized, so investors should treat the disclosed numbers as estimates rather than final outcomes.

FDCTech notes that it has filed all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months, suggesting that the delay is specific to the complexity of this acquisition rather than a broader compliance breakdown. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Financial Officer and Principal Financial Officer Imran Firoz, dated March 30, 2026, underscoring management’s formal responsibility for the delayed filing and the ongoing effort to complete it promptly.

The most recent analyst rating on (FDCT) stock is a Hold with a $0.02 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FDCTech stock, see the FDCT Stock Forecast page.

These risks are partially offset by a stable, low-leverage balance sheet, strong revenue growth, a low P/E valuation, and positive expansion-related corporate events.

To see Spark’s full report on FDCT stock, click here.

data.description:

IGC Pharma ( (IGC) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, IGC Pharma announced a 12-part strategic national media partnership with financial media platform New to The Street to bolster its investor relations and corporate communications. The collaboration aims to expand visibility among institutional and retail investors through long-form televised interviews, national TV commercials, digital campaigns, and outdoor billboards in key financial hubs.

The campaign will feature IGC Pharma on sponsored programming on Fox Business and Bloomberg Television, with additional reach via digital distribution to more than 4.45 million YouTube subscribers, outdoor placements in Times Square and the New York City Financial District, and earned media across major U.S. broadcast affiliates. By tapping into New to The Street’s integrated television, digital, social, and event-based investor engagement network, IGC seeks to broaden market awareness and strengthen its capital markets profile while emphasizing continued focus on clinical execution and scientific development.

More about IGC Pharma

IGC Pharma, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing AI-enabled therapeutics for Alzheimer’s disease and metabolic disorders. Its lead asset, IGC-AD1, is in a Phase 2 CALMA trial targeting agitation in Alzheimer’s dementia, supported by a pipeline including TGR-63 for amyloid plaques and early-stage programs in neurodegeneration, tau, and metabolic dysfunctions.

The company leverages artificial intelligence to accelerate drug discovery, optimize clinical trials, and refine patient targeting, underpinned by a broad patent portfolio. Operating under the ticker NYSE American: IGC, it positions itself as an innovator advancing breakthrough therapies in neurology and related metabolic conditions.

data.title: Skillsoft Faces NYSE Noncompliance, Pursues Compliance Plan
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Skillsoft ( (SKIL) ).

On March 30, 2026, Skillsoft disclosed that it received a NYSE notice on March 26 stating the company is not in compliance with Section 802.01B listing standards, after its 30‑day average market capitalization and stockholders’ equity each fell below $50 million. Skillsoft has up to 18 months, subject to an approved business plan to be submitted within 45 days, to cure the deficiency, during which its shares will remain listed and trading, and the company emphasized that the noncompliance does not affect ongoing operations or SEC reporting obligations.

Management said it will evaluate all available alternatives to restore compliance, a process that will be monitored through quarterly NYSE reviews and could prompt strategic, capital or balance sheet actions. The notice underscores pressure on Skillsoft’s valuation and equity base but preserves near-term trading continuity for shareholders while the company seeks to regain its standing on the exchange.

data.title: Dolly Varden Silver Delisted After Completing All-Share Merger With Contango
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Dolly Varden Silver ( (TSE:DV) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Dolly Varden Silver Corporation and Contango Silver & Gold Inc. completed their previously announced merger via a statutory plan of arrangement under British Columbia law, following all required shareholder and court approvals. Under the deal, all outstanding Dolly Varden shares were acquired by an indirect Contango subsidiary, with investors receiving 0.1652 Contango share or equivalent exchangeable share for each Dolly Varden share, and all Dolly Varden stock options converted into replacement Contango securities.

As part of the transaction, Contango issued 13,686,278 common shares, replacement options for 417,048 shares and its subsidiary issued 1,597,301 exchangeable shares to former Dolly Varden securityholders. Dolly Varden’s shares were delisted from the TSX Venture Exchange at the close of trading on March 27, 2026 and ceased trading on the NYSE American and Frankfurt exchanges, while Contango has moved to secure a Toronto Stock Exchange listing, consolidating its capital markets presence under a leadership team led by CEO Rick Van Nieuwenhuyse and President Shawn Khunkhun.

The combined company’s board now includes representatives from both sides, including Chairman Clynt Nauman and executives with deep mining industry expertise, signaling a governance structure designed to integrate Dolly Varden’s assets into Contango’s broader portfolio. For existing Dolly Varden shareholders, the merger marks a shift from holding a standalone silver explorer to owning equity in a larger precious metals vehicle, with future liquidity and valuation tied to Contango’s performance and its planned TSX listing.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:DV) stock is a Sell with a C$3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dolly Varden Silver stock, see the TSE:DV Stock Forecast page.

The score is mainly pressured by weak financial quality (no revenue, widening losses, and rising cash burn) and bearish technical signals (trading below major moving averages with weak momentum). A debt-free balance sheet provides some offset, but valuation signals are limited due to negative earnings and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on DV stock, click here.

Dolly Varden Silver Corporation is a Canadian precious metals company focused on silver exploration and development, historically listed on the TSX Venture Exchange, NYSE American and Frankfurt Stock Exchange. The company operated in the mining sector, with its shares providing investors exposure to silver-focused assets prior to its acquisition by Contango Silver & Gold Inc.

data.title: Kirby Expands and Extends Revolving Credit Facility Commitment
data.description:

Kirby ( (KEX) ) has shared an update.

On March 26, 2026, Kirby Corporation amended and restated its main bank credit facility, extending its maturity to March 26, 2031 and increasing revolving commitments to $750 million while eliminating the prior term loan component. The agreement preserves existing financial covenants, including minimum interest coverage and a cap on debt to capitalization, and allows potential upsizing by up to an additional $500 million subject to lender approval.

At the effectiveness of the new facility, Kirby had about $200 million drawn, using the proceeds to refinance debt under the prior agreement, fund vessel equipment acquisitions, and support share repurchases, and subsequently repaid $20 million before the filing date. The revised structure provides Kirby with longer-dated, more flexible liquidity to support fleet investment and capital returns, potentially strengthening its balance sheet resilience and financial flexibility for future growth and shareholder-focused actions.

On March 24, 2026, Commerce Bancshares, Inc. announced that Corporate Controller and Chief Accounting Officer Paul A. Steiner had resigned to pursue other opportunities, with the company emphasizing that his departure did not stem from any disagreement over financial, accounting or operational matters. data.title: Sativus Tech Delays Annual 10-K Yearly Filing
data.description:
SATIVUS TECH CORP ( (SATT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sativus Tech Corp. has notified regulators that it will be late filing its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, via a Form 12b-25 notice. The delayed filing relates specifically to the company’s Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report), not to a Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) or other periodic forms.

The company attributes the delay to events it describes as unforeseen, which have prevented completion of the annual report without incurring what it considers unreasonable effort and expense. No additional detail is provided on whether these are operational issues, accounting complexities, or external constraints such as third-party or auditor timing.

Sativus Tech Corp. states that it expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the automatic extension window allowed under SEC rules. Specifically, it plans to submit the annual report no later than the fifteenth calendar day following the original due date, which is the standard grace period triggered by a Form 12b-25 filing.

The company indicates that it does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year as will be reflected in the earnings statements included in the delayed report. Investors should note that this expectation is inherently forward-looking and remains subject to normal reporting adjustments once the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is finalized and filed.

Sativus Tech Corp. confirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling ongoing baseline compliance despite the current delay. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Michael Oster on March 30, 2026, underscoring management’s formal responsibility for the disclosure and its stated plan to complete the filing within the permitted extension.

On March 24, 2026, Champions Oncology, Inc., a global oncology-focused research services provider, reported that its Board accepted the resignation of director Dr. Philip Breitfeld and appointed Brian Alexander, MD, MPH, to fill the resulting vacancy. The company noted that Breitfeld’s departure did not stem from any disagreement over operations or policies, and that there are no special arrangements or related-party transactions tied to Alexander’s appointment.

Announced publicly on March 30, 2026, the move underscores Champions’ push to deepen its data-driven oncology strategy by linking its large portfolio of patient-derived tumor models with rich molecular and clinical datasets. data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

WPP ( (WPP) ) has shared an update.

On March 30, 2026, WPP plc filed a Form 6-K in the United States, formally incorporating a new base indenture for debt securities and related guarantees into its existing shelf registration. The documentation, involving WPP 2025 LLC, WPP Finance 2010 and several guarantor subsidiaries, sets the legal framework under which WPP can issue future series of guaranteed notes in the U.S. market, defining trustee arrangements, enforcement rights and covenants that will govern creditors’ claims.

The filing, backed by legal opinions under New York, English and Jersey law, strengthens WPP’s capital markets infrastructure and provides flexibility to structure and tap additional debt as needed. By putting a comprehensive, Trust Indenture Act-compliant platform in place with Wilmington Trust as trustee and Citibank as registrar and paying agent, WPP enhances its ability to access U.S. investors efficiently and standardize terms across future note issuances, which is significant for funding strategy and bondholder protections.

The latest announcement is out from Ferrari ( (IT:RACE) ).

Ferrari reported on March 30, 2026 that it continued executing the first €250 million tranche of its multi-year share buyback program, which is expected to total about €3.5 billion by 2030. Between March 23 and March 27, 2026, the company repurchased 107,859 common shares on Euronext Milan and the NYSE for roughly €30.1 million, signaling an ongoing commitment to shareholder returns and balance-sheet optimization.

Since the first tranche was announced on December 16, 2025 and the broader buyback launched on January 5, 2026, Ferrari has acquired 737,600 own shares on both exchanges for a cumulative €217.2 million. As of March 27, 2026, Ferrari held 17,382,206 common shares in treasury, equal to 8.96% of issued common shares, underscoring a sizeable reduction in free float that could support earnings per share and reinforce the company’s market positioning in the luxury automotive sector.

Kinross Gold ( (TSE:K) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Kinross Gold Corporation has furnished a Form 6-K to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission dated March 30, 2026, in connection with its regulatory reporting obligations as a foreign private issuer. The filing formally incorporates the company’s latest Form S-8 registration statements by reference and confirms that Kinross continues to use the U.S. disclosure framework to support its share-based compensation programs.

The Form 6-K’s stated purpose is to provide U.S. investors with access to Kinross’s 2025 Annual Report, which includes audited financial statements, notes, and management discussion and analysis for the year ended December 31, 2025. By furnishing this report, Kinross aligns its U.S. disclosure with its SEDAR+ filing in Canada, offering shareholders and analysts a consistent, cross-border view of its 2025 financial performance and governance, though the detailed financial results are contained in the underlying annual report rather than this notice.
